context.projectOverview: agent-bober: multi-agent harness building apps autonomously. This plan adds Claude Code Dynamic Workflows as a third, config-selected orchestration engine (pipeline.engine) behind the frozen runPipeline seam. Sprint 1 introduces the seam, the pipeline.engine config field (default 'ts'), the PipelineEngine interface, a PipelineEngineSelector mirroring checkpoints/registry.ts resolution, and a default-ineligible eligibility probe. The TS engine must wrap the existing runPipeline body with ZERO behavior change.

principles: See .bober/principles.md — TS strict zero-error, ESLint zero-error, ESM with .js import specifiers, Zod for config, no synchronous fs, consistent-type-imports, conventional commits. READ IT.
notes: Architecture seam is at src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:515 (exported runPipeline). The selector pattern to mirror is src/orchestrator/checkpoints/registry.ts:65 (resolveCheckpointMechanismName — pure resolver + lookup). PipelineResult is defined at src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:62 and must be reused verbatim. Do NOT change any agents/*.md, the contracts protocol, or providers/types.ts. The full unit suite has a documented flaky tool-count timeout baseline (~2 failures) that is tolerated.
